name: pyproteins
documentation:
#


## Development purpose

```sh
git clone https://github.com/glaunay/pyproteins.git  LOCAL_PATH_TO_REPO
```

Check for dependencies in `LOCAL_PATH_TO_REPO/setup.py`

```python
sys.path.append(LOCAL_PATH_TO_REPO/src)
```

## Deployment module

```sh
pip install pyproteins
```

## Update pip module
 TO DO

### modules organization

#### container modules

##### Core
Frequently used container and functions.
Downloading and parsing XML in a generic way.
Two-level dictionnaries.

##### customCollection

Managment of a collection of object which can be constructed from flat files (ex: uniprot).

* collectionPath: path to a directory of flat files
* constructor: object constructor
* indexer function to map filename and collection key

#### sequence modules

##### msa

* Load alignemnt files
* Filter alignment
* Compute statistics

##### peptide
Fasta framed informations, like amino-acid types, domain boundaries and secondary structure states.

##### psipred
DEPRECATED

#### sequence alignment Modules

* nw_custom: the Needleman & Wunsch implementaion
* scoringFunctions : AA, AA/SS2, PWA
* scoringMatrix : Blosum62

#### utils modules

test Cecile

methods.name: pyproteins.alignment.scoringFunctions.frosto1D.setScore
documentation: We choose to discard gap from counts

functions.name: pyproteins.sequence.psipred.parse
documentation: Not intended to make psipred calls
for now we only parse the horiz file

documentation: import numba #--> speed up vector matrix walks
scorer must return float given a pair of entity item

documentation: jit decorator tells Numba to compile this function.

classes.name: pyproteins.container.Core.mdTree

methods.name: pyproteins.container.Core.mdTree.__getitem__
documentation: We unroll to copy ref of collection elements not of collection itself
# Two lvl tree, top/down key are min/max md5 hash

classes.name: pyproteins.container.Core.dnTree

methods.name: pyproteins.container.Core.dnTree.getNonDense
documentation: Get All elements connected to k1, w/ weight consideration
# Minimal number of top level leaves tree
# Wrapper to md5 tree with dynamic access to content

functions.name: pyproteins.sequence.msa.seqPairScores
documentation: Quick and dirty quality assesment of the coverage of one sequence w/ respect to the MASTER
no gap is expected in master ie 'seqOne'
We dont do a pairwise optimal sequence alignment, just read straight from the msa

functions.name: pyproteins.sequence.msa.map
documentation: will return a vector of msaRef column numbers, with a value for the
eventual corresponding column number in msaShrink
TODO: wrap&use hmmr to align the two msa by their profiles

classes.name: pyproteins.sequence.msa.MsaBean
documentation: We need to fill a matrix of distances sized to the query msa

methods.name: pyproteins.sequence.msa.Msa.frequency
documentation: Putting frequency attribute in lazy instantiation leads to a factor x4 constructor time

methods.name: pyproteins.sequence.msa.Msa.maskMaster
documentation: Strip the MSA from any colmuns where specified master sequence features a gap

methods.name: pyproteins.sequence.msa.Msa.recordLookup
documentation: retrieve a sequence in the msa, by index or regular expression

methods.name: pyproteins.sequence.msa.Msa.masterCoverage
documentation: Just count the sim/id  and coverage(non-gap) of each sequence related to master

modules.name: pyproteins.utils.make_json_serializable
documentation:
Module that monkey-patches json module when it's imported so
JSONEncoder.default() automatically checks for a special "toJSON()"
method and uses it to encode the object if found.
